methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Photoroom, an AI & Software company founded by Matthieu Rouif and Eliot Andres, is a Series B stage enterprise currently valued at $500M post-money on $43M raised. The peer universe consists of four same-stage, same-sector comparables: Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), and Nominal ($1.0B), supplemented by one public comparable (Palantir) and additional private benchmarks.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $500M last post-money, yielding $950M. Peer-median analysis across the four same-sector Series B companies produces a median valuation of $5.3B, though the peer range spans $1.0B to $32.0B, reflecting material heterogeneity within the cohort. The wide spread reflects both company-specific execution risk and the nascent nature of frontier AI valuations.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $808M (low) to $6.1B (high), with a median of $3.1B. The current $500M valuation sits below the low-end anchor, suggesting either a significant discount to peer equivalents or meaningful execution uncertainties not captured in public comps. Key uncertainties include heterogeneity within the peer set—Safe Superintelligence commands a 32x premium over Nominal—and the limited relevance of traditional software metrics to frontier AI businesses. Stage-progression and peer-median divergence ($950M versus $5.3B) reflects fundamental difficulty in comparable-company valuation for early-stage AI ventures.
